rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $629,540.00 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 15, 2025, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Default Judgment. Petitioner People of the State of California's unopposed Motion For Default Judgment is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court will enter default judgment against Respondent Property $616,680.00 U.S. Currency and declare the Respondent Property forfeited pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 11470 et seq. and order respondent Property distributed pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 11489. The court will enter the proposed order/judgment submitted with the moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: JENNY PEREZ VS. PONTES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Intervene On Behalf Of Defendants Silas Bezerra And Luiz Paixao
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 15, 2025, Line 4, OTHER FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Intervene On Behalf Of Defendants Silas Bezerra And Luiz Paixao. Farmers Insurance Exchange's unopposed Motion to Intervene is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court grants Farmer Insurance Exchange leave to intervene as a defendant in this action. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 387(d)(1)(B).) Farmer Insurance Exchange shall file its answer in intervention forthwith, but no later than 20 days after entry of this order. Farmer Insurance Exchange should present this order at the time it submits its answer in intervention for filing. FIE must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 8:30 am on 7/15/2025 a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
